channel.title: MacwanGrid — Blog
channel.description: Notes on infrastructure, cybersecurity, AI, and leadership from a practitioner's desk.

item.title: Why I Built a Dark Web Research Site (and What I Learned)
item.description: A year of studying privacy engineering by building one. Lessons on Whonix, Tor hidden services, and operational isolation.

item.title: The CISA KEV Catalog as a Prioritized Patch Backlog
item.description: Most patch programs drown in CVEs. KEV is the strongest signal most teams aren't using well. Here's how.

item.title: AI in IT Operations: A Reality Check
item.description: What actually works, what's vendor theater, and where I think the genuine leverage lives over the next two years.

item.title: MIT Week 1: Back in a Classroom After 20 Years
item.description: A personal reflection on returning to formal study after two decades of practice.

item.title: What To Do In The First 24 Hours
item.description: The calm-first playbook: isolate, preserve, engage legal, out-of-band comms — and the things you must NOT do.

item.title: Home Security Lab: The Minimum Viable Stack
item.description: What the lab actually needs: hypervisor, routed VLAN, EDR trials, AD test forest, packet capture.

item.title: Zero Trust Is Harder Than Vendors Admit
item.description: The concept is sound. Implementations are often theatrical. A pragmatic path through the Zero Trust journey.

item.title: Reading the Threat Intelligence Landscape
item.description: Threat intel has a tier system nobody writes down. Strategic, operational, tactical, technical.

item.title: LLM-Powered Runbooks: Where They Work
item.description: LLMs augment runbooks well, but only after you've stopped treating them as answer engines.

item.title: The 10-Point Ransomware Readiness Checklist
item.description: Most organizations discover their gaps after the encryption starts. The ten things to have in place before.

item.title: OSINT for IT Leaders
item.description: Free OSINT sources give you 80% of what costs $100k+ in vendor subscriptions.

item.title: Kubernetes for Enterprise IT (Not Just Developers)
item.description: Kubernetes is infrastructure, not a dev platform. Teams who let it sit with product teams regret it.

item.title: Setting Up Whonix: Step by Step
item.description: Windows + VirtualBox + Whonix. Install, bootstrap Tor, first onion service, common pitfalls.

item.title: US Cyber Authorities: Who to Call
item.description: FBI IC3, CISA, Secret Service, state AG, SEC. What each does, when to call, how to report.

item.title: Tor for IT Professionals: A Primer
item.description: Most IT leaders think Tor = illegal and stop there. It's a critical privacy tool used by journalists and researchers.

item.title: MFA Is Necessary. It's Not Sufficient.
item.description: MFA is table stakes. Adversaries have moved to MFA fatigue, session hijacking, OAuth abuse.

item.title: Prompt Injection: The OWASP of AI
item.description: Prompt injection is the SQL injection of 2026. Most teams haven't even mapped their attack surface yet.

item.title: Dark Web Intel: What Actually Matters
item.description: Most dark-web-monitoring products oversell. Only two classes of findings actually change defender behavior.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Multi-Cloud
item.description: Multi-cloud for resilience is often more fragile than single-cloud. Expertise duplication, data gravity, egress fees.

item.title: Whonix Architecture Explained
item.description: Whonix separates the application from the network at the VM boundary. A beautiful piece of engineering.

item.title: Local LLM for SOC Prototyping
item.description: Ollama + Llama on a dedicated box. Why local matters for prototyping, what you can't prototype locally.

item.title: Tabletop Exercises That Actually Stress Your Plan
item.description: Most tabletops are theatre. A good one sends you back to documentation with 3-5 gaps to fix.

item.title: Credit Freezes: Step by Step
item.description: All three bureaus, walked through. Child credit freezes. When to lift. Fraud alerts vs. freezes.

item.title: RAG for Internal Documentation
item.description: RAG over internal docs is the most boring, most useful enterprise AI pattern.

item.title: Observability vs Monitoring: A Practitioner's Take
item.description: Monitoring answers what you thought to ask. Observability answers questions you didn't anticipate.

item.title: Building an Intel-Driven Security Program
item.description: Most SOCs claim intel-driven but operate alert-driven. What actually changes when intel drives detection.

item.title: EDR Alone Isn't Enough
item.description: EDR is great at endpoints. Attackers moved up (identity) and out (SaaS, OT). What else you need.

item.title: Hidden Service Fundamentals
item.description: Hidden services are misunderstood. A powerful primitive for authenticated, end-to-end encrypted services.

item.title: Proxmox vs ESXi in 2026
item.description: Post-Broadcom ESXi pricing. Proxmox for home + mid-enterprise. Feature parity for 80%, honest gaps.

item.title: AI Tier-1 SOC Triage: Six-Month Results
item.description: After six months of LLM-assisted tier-1 triage, here's what I'd tell other IT leaders.

item.title: Identity Theft Recovery Workflow
item.description: IdentityTheft.gov five-step process, police reports, credit bureau disputes, tax-related ID theft.

item.title: Data Platform Maturity: From Lake to Lakehouse
item.description: The data lake promise failed. Lakehouse (Iceberg, Delta) succeeds where it does.

item.title: Vendor Threat Feeds: What You're Paying For
item.description: The $500k feed has 85% overlap with the $10k one. The real value is often elsewhere.

item.title: Insider Threat: The Uncomfortable Conversation
item.description: Most insider threats aren't malicious. They're privilege sprawl, bad offboarding, and ego.

item.title: Ethics of Dark Web Research
item.description: Doing security research on Tor requires a code. Legal research vs. crossing lines.

item.title: Hardening Your Home Network
item.description: Separate IoT VLAN, DNS filtering, WPA3, guest network, Wireguard. Practical list.

item.title: Executive Breach Response Playbook
item.description: Board notification, crisis comms, counsel, insurance, regulators. The 48-72h window.

item.title: Why Your SRE Team Isn't Scaling
item.description: SRE doesn't scale by hiring more SREs. It scales by reducing toil and raising abstraction.

item.title: Model Selection for Enterprise IT
item.description: The LLM you pick for enterprise IT isn't the one on the ChatGPT homepage.

item.title: The Five Sources I Read Every Morning
item.description: A 20-minute curated read-in beats a 2-hour dashboard crawl.

item.title: Vulnerability Management That Isn't a CSV Dump
item.description: You don't need to patch everything. You need to patch what adversaries are exploiting.

item.title: Identity Separation in Security Work
item.description: If you do research under a pseudonym, your clearnet and shadow identities need actual separation.

item.title: Bitwarden Enterprise: 18-Month Review
item.description: What works, what's rough. SSO + team shares (good). SCIM nuances + mobile UX (uneven).

item.title: Endpoint Management: Zero Trust for Devices
item.description: Your endpoint is the new perimeter segment. Managing it like it's 2015 is why you're breached.

item.title: GDPR Breach Notification Requirements
item.description: 72-hour clock, DPA notification, when "high risk" triggers data subject notification.

item.title: Data Leakage in AI Workflows
item.description: Every AI workflow is a new data egress vector. Most orgs don't think about it until the lawyers find out.

item.title: Attribution: When It Matters
item.description: Attribution is overrated for defenders and underrated for response.

item.title: Breach Notification in the US: State-by-State Reality
item.description: There's no federal breach notification law. Every state has its own.

item.title: 1Password vs KeePassXC vs Bitwarden
item.description: Honest comparison. 1Password UX best. KeePassXC best for sovereignty. Bitwarden best value.

item.title: Network Architecture for Hybrid Work
item.description: The VPN-everywhere model broke during COVID. What modern hybrid-work architecture looks like.

item.title: PGP for Technical Leaders
item.description: PGP has a bad UX reputation. For technical leaders it's a useful credential beyond encryption.

item.title: Ransomware Negotiation: When NOT to Pay
item.description: OFAC sanctions lists, decryptor existence, insurance position.

item.title: The Economics of GenAI in the Enterprise
item.description: The ROI on GenAI isn't where the vendor deck says it is.

item.title: Signal vs Noise: The Security News Firehose
item.description: There's too much security news. 95% doesn't change what you do tomorrow.

item.title: Cloud Cost Optimization: The Framework
item.description: FinOps isn't a team. It's a practice. The biggest wins are in design, not dashboards.

item.title: Personal OSINT Workstation Build
item.description: Compartmentalized browsers, VPN stack, Maltego free tier, SpiderFoot, case file workflow.

item.title: SOC Maturity: The Plateaus Nobody Warns You About
item.description: Most SOCs plateau at proactive hunt and detection engineering.

item.title: Business Email Compromise Recovery
item.description: Wire fraud specifics, IC3 within 72h, the Financial Fraud Kill Chain.

item.title: Running a Warrant Canary
item.description: A canary is a signaling mechanism, not a legal defense.

item.title: Shadow AI: What IT Doesn't Know
item.description: If you haven't counted Shadow AI usage, it's higher than you think.

item.title: Threat Landscape Q1 2026: Patterns
item.description: A quarterly practitioner's read on what actually changed.

item.title: Identity as the New Perimeter
item.description: Network perimeter is dead. Identity is the new perimeter.

item.title: The Home Lab Threat Model
item.description: The lab IS internet-exposed whether you mean it to be or not.

item.title: Breach Disclosure Letter Templates
item.description: Consumer, employee, regulatory templates. Real language.

item.title: Privacy-Respecting Logging Patterns
item.description: You can run a useful service without logging things that would identify your users.

item.title: A Practitioner's Patching Priority Framework
item.description: CVSS alone won't cut it. A four-factor model.

item.title: Agentic AI: Hype vs. Production Reality
item.description: Agentic AI is real but fragile. What works, what breaks.

item.title: MITRE ATT&CK for Defenders
item.description: ATT&CK is a framework, not a checklist.

item.title: The Case for Boring Technology
item.description: Novel tech is expensive in ways that don't show up in a POC.

item.title: From Home Lab to Production: Lessons
item.description: What translates, what doesn't.

item.title: Reading the Dark Web: Intel Sources
item.description: What a practitioner actually reads and how they use it.

item.title: Supply Chain Breach: Your Responsibilities
item.description: When your vendor gets breached. Contract review, shared-responsibility, customer notification.

item.title: Supply Chain Security: Five Years After SolarWinds
item.description: SolarWinds shifted the conversation. Five years later, most orgs still don't have real supply chain controls.

item.title: Evaluating AI Vendors: The Practitioner's Checklist
item.description: Every AI vendor deck looks the same. The ten questions that separate real from fake.

item.title: Onion Services v3: An Operator's Notes
item.description: Running a v3 onion service isn't hard technically. The operational discipline is where people fail.
